text: I feel I am a person of worth, at least on an equal basis with others.
I feel that I have a number of good qualities.
All in all, I am inclined to feel that I am a failure.
I am able to do things as well as most other people.
I feel I do not have much to be proud of.
I take a positive attitude toward myself.
On the whole, I am satisfied with myself.
I wish I could have more respect for myself.
I certainly feel useless at times.
At times I think I am no good at all.
When I make a mistake, I treat myself kindly.
When I’m struggling, I comfort myself with warmth.
When I think about my flaws, I feel disconnected from others.
When painful feelings arise, I can observe them as they are.
When I fail, I criticize myself harshly.
I understand that everyone sometimes feels inadequate.
I am my own biggest supporter.
When I feel upset, I tell myself, “It’s okay.”
My problems feel like mine alone, and that makes me lonely.
In hard times, I give myself what I need.
I only feel valuable when I succeed.
I only feel good when someone praises me.
Even if I fail, my worth does not change.
I am precious regardless of my appearance or abilities.
Other people’s evaluations shake my self-esteem a lot.
I am valuable simply by existing, no matter what I do.
If I don’t get good results, I feel worthless.
My worth is separate from my achievements.
Whenever I’m compared to someone, my sense of value wavers.
I am enough as I am.
My abilities can improve with effort.
Failure is a learning opportunity for me.
My intelligence is fixed and I can’t really change it.
Difficult tasks help me grow.
I am someone who keeps developing.
I enjoy the process of learning something new.
Criticism is information that helps me become better.
My personality is almost impossible to change.
Mistakes are evidence of my growth.
A year from now, I will be better than I am today.
I feel comfortable being with other people.
I worry that people may not like me.
I can express my opinions confidently.
It’s hard to be myself in front of others.
In relationships, I feel my worth is recognized.
I feel like I am a burden to others.
If people knew the real me, they would be disappointed.
I feel assertive in my relationships with others.
Even when I’m alone, I believe I’m still a good person.
I am worthy of love.
